creator: exploringlondon
caption:
There is something strange about this street in London 👀 Look closely when you walk down Leinster Gardens near Bayswater, at house number 23 and 24. No doorbells, no letterboxes and the windows are all blocked out.

Why? Go around the back to Porchester Terrace to find out!
You will see that they are actually fake houses, just facades hiding a section of underground railway, that was originally built in the 1860s, and these open spaces on the line were used for ventilation to allow steam to escape from the original steam trains! 🚂

📍23-24 Leinster Gardens, London W2 3BH

transcription.text: This might just look like a normal street in central London, but as you walk down you might notice something strange about some of the houses. This is Lannister Gardens and there's something odd about house number 23 and 24. At first glance they look like normal houses, but take a closer look. There's no doorbell, there's no letterbox, the windows are all blocked out and the answer to why this is lies in the street behind. Walk around to Portchester Terrace, peep over the wall and you'll find that these are fake houses. They are fake facades covering a section of underground railway. This was used as a ventilation point back when the first underground trains in London used steam in the 1860s. Today trains still run along these tracks, part of the circle and district lines between Paddington and Bayswater. Most people walking past wouldn't even know. Find the exact location in the caption.
